definitions.definition: A state in which the concentrations of reactants and products do not change with time. In this state, the rate of forward reaction equals the rate of reverse reaction.
definitions.example: The equilibrium is reached in a reversible reaction where rate of forward reaction = rate of backward reaction.

definitions.definition: A state of equilibrium where the forward and backward reactions occur simultaneously.
definitions.example: The equilibrium between water and its vapors is a dynamic equilibrium.

definitions.definition: The reaction in which the products of a reaction convert to the reactants.
definitions.example: Hydrogen + iodine ⇌ 2 HI (reverse reaction: 2 HI ⇌ Hydrogen + iodine)

definitions.definition: The reaction in which the reactants convert to the products.
A numerical value that expresses the ratio of concentrations of products to the concentrations of reactants at equilibrium.
definitions.example: Equilibrium constant (Kc) = [C] [D] / [A] [B] for the reaction A + B ⇌ C + D

definitions.definition: A type of equilibrium in which the reactants and products are in different phases.
definitions.example: The equilibrium between a solid (CaO) and its aqueous solution is a heterogeneous equilibrium.

definitions.definition: A type of equilibrium in which the reactants and products are in the same phase.
definitions.example: The equilibrium between a solution of NH3 and N2 is a homogeneous equilibrium.

definitions.definition: A numerical value that expresses the ratio of concentrations of products to the concentrations of reactants.
A mixture that is at equilibrium.
definitions.example: The equilibrium mixture of a solution of NH3 and N2 is at equilibrium.

definitions.definition: A principle that states that when a system at equilibrium is subjected to a change in concentration, temperature, or pressure, the equilibrium will shift in a direction that tends to counteract the change.
definitions.example: When a change in concentration is made, the equilibrium will shift to counteract the change.

definitions.definition: An expression that contains the equilibrium constant (Kc) and the concentrations of reactants and products at equilibrium.

definitions.term: Equilibrium Constant for a Homogeneous Reaction
definitions.definition: A numerical value that expresses the ratio of concentrations of products to the concentrations of reactants at equilibrium for a homogeneous reaction.
definitions.example: Kc = [C] [D] / [A] [B] for the reaction A + B ⇌ C + D

definitions.term: Equilibrium Constant for a Heterogeneous Reaction
definitions.definition: A numerical value that expresses the ratio of concentrations of products to the concentrations of reactants at equilibrium for a heterogeneous reaction.
definitions.example: Kc = [C] for the reaction CaO ⇌ Ca + O2

definitions.definition: A numerical value that expresses the ratio of concentrations of products to the concentrations of reactants at equilibrium.
definitions.example: Kc = 10 for the reaction A + B ⇌ C + D

definitions.definition: A principle that states that the rate of a chemical reaction is proportional to the product of the concentrations of the reactants.
definitions.example: The rate of a reaction is proportional to the product of concentrations of reactants.

definitions.definition: A state in which the concentrations of reactants and products do not change with time.
definitions.example: The equilibrium between water and its vapors is a chemical equilibrium.

definitions.definition: A state in which the physical properties of a system do not change with time.
definitions.example: The equilibrium between solid CO2 and its vapors is a physical equilibrium.
